role_title: Technology Program Manager - Assistant Vice President

level_signals: Leads end-to-end delivery of complex cross-functional programs

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience in at least two of the following: Technical Program Management, Financial services industry, Technology or financial services consulting
Strong understanding of software development lifecycle (SDLC) methodologies
Familiarity with relational database concepts and data modeling
Experience with project management tools such as JIRA, Monday.com, or similar platforms
Ability to influence stakeholders and build consensus across diverse viewpoints
Strategic thinker who can connect big-picture objectives to detailed execution plans
Excellent verbal and written communication skills

This role requires comfort operating in an AI-augmented environment — identifying opportunities to use emerging technologies to improve planning, reporting, decision-making, and cross-team coordination.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Business Administration, or related field preferred

explicitly_excluded: hands-on coding or testing (explicitly stated: 'it does not involve hands-on coding or testing')

scope.surface: Cross-functional technology programs spanning greenfield software development, enterprise vendor implementations, cloud and infrastructure, data & analytics, machine learning, and technology innovation
primary_metrics_inferred: On-time delivery of complex technology programs
core_responsibilities: Lead end-to-end delivery of complex, cross-functional technology programs spanning multiple engineering teams
Partner with Engineering to define scope, roadmap, milestones, and measurable outcomes
Establish clear governance, reporting, and risk management practices to ensure transparency and accountability
Communicate program status, risks, dependencies, and mitigation strategies to senior stakeholders
Collaborate with Technology leadership to shape forward-looking strategy and execution frameworks
Drive continuous improvement initiatives to enhance delivery effectiveness across the broader Technology organization
Bring structure and clarity to ambiguous or evolving initiatives

compensation.equity_form: equity for all full-time employees (form not specified)

green_flags: Fully remote role explicitly stated in JD body
Equity offered for all full-time employees
Clear scope: does not involve hands-on coding/testing — well-defined for a TPM role
Diverse program portfolio (greenfield dev, cloud, ML, vendor implementations) signals broad learning opportunities
AI-augmented environment signals forward-looking technology culture
red_flags: PMP and degree are listed as 'preferred/plus' yet core experience requirements include multiple overlapping domains — overall bar may be higher than stated
AVP title used but level calibration varies significantly across financial services firms — seniority ambiguous

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from AVP title + years range + leadership scope)
scope.business_model (inferred from iCapital's known fintech/wealthtech positioning)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
ambiguities: The qualifications list four domains ('Technical Program Management, Financial services industry, Technology or financial services consulting') but the preamble says 'at least two of the following' — the JD only lists three distinct domains (not four as formatted), creating minor structural ambiguity about what constitutes distinct categories.
Compensation described qualitatively ('competitive salary, annual performance bonus, equity') but no ranges are provided — classified as 'unknown' source.
AVP title is a financial-services convention that does not map cleanly to IC vs. manager level; treated as Senior IC based on absence of direct reports language.
validation_issues: must_have two_of_four_domains: raw_text_from_jd not found in JD even after bullet/whitespace normalization
